reasoning: Cosponsored H.R. 3069 (119th Congress) to establish Medicare for All. Previously coauthored AB 1400, California's single-payer Guaranteed Health Care for All bill, as a state senator. Her campaign website lists "Health care for all" as a core priority.

Former Planned Parenthood Los Angeles board member. Cosponsored H.R. 12 (Women's Health Protection Act), H.R. 4611 (affordable abortion coverage), H.R. 4099 (prohibit state interference with interstate abortion services), and H.R. 2029 (protect medication abortions) in the 119th Congress.

Criticized Trump's broad tariffs on India as "incredibly unfortunate" (October 2025 IANS interview) and called the administration's trade approach "reckless" on the House floor (September 2025). Co-led a letter with 18 Democrats urging Trump to "reset and repair" trade relationships. Favors strategic trade engagement over broad tariffs.

Congressional Progressive Caucus member who celebrated the Inflation Reduction Act for "raising taxes on the wealthy and corporations." Criticized the Republican One Big Beautiful Bill for giving "a tax break to billionaires like Elon Musk." Consistently opposes Republican tax cuts for the wealthy and supports IRS enforcement against wealthy tax cheats.

Member of the Congressional Equality Caucus. Cosponsored the PRIDE Act of 2025 (H.R. 4163) to ensure same-sex couples receive full tax benefits. In June 2023 statement on 303 Creative LLC v. Elenis, called for enacting the Equality Act and referenced the Respect for Marriage Act. Supported California's ballot measure enshrining marriage equality.

In June 2023, criticized the 303 Creative LLC v. Elenis ruling allowing religious-based refusal of service to LGBTQ+ individuals, saying the Court "fanned the flames of hate, division, and bigotry." Called for enacting the
Equality Act, which prohibits discrimination based on sexual orientation and gender identity without broad religious exemptions.

Congressional Equality Caucus member who has consistently opposed "extremist legislation determined to roll back protections and threaten the safety and security of LGBTQ+ individuals" (June 2023 Pride statement). Calls for enacting the Equality Act extending civil rights protections to gender identity. Pattern of strong LGBTQ+ advocacy supports this position.

Cosponsored H.Res. 155 (119th Congress) reaffirming support for Ukraine's sovereignty. On MSNBC (late 2023), stated U.S. "national security is also dependent upon our ability and willingness to continue to support our allies." Voted for H.R. 8038 (21st Century Peace through
Strength Act, April 2024) which included Ukraine assistance.

Cosponsored H.R. 3069 (119th Congress) to establish Medicare for All.
In California, coauthored AB 1400 for single-payer healthcare. Voted against a 2025 continuing resolution citing 58000 constituents at risk of losing Medicaid/Medicare/ACA coverage. Also cosponsored H.R. 4796 to repeal prohibition on certain Medicaid payments.

Campaign website lists "A Green New Deal and immediate climate action" as a priority. Received 100% Sierra Club score (2020) as a CA Assemblymember. Celebrated the Inflation Reduction Act's clean energy investments. Coauthored the Plastic Pollution Reduction Act (SB 54/AB 1080) in California. Green New Deal framework envisions stopping new fossil fuel permits.

Cosponsored H.R. 14, the John R. Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act (2025). Campaign lists "Expanding voting rights" as a top priority. Received an "A" from End Citizens United/Let America Vote.
In California, advocated for restoring voting rights to parolees.

Introduced the LINE Act (H.R. 5319, Limiting ICE's National Encroachment Act) to block federal agencies from sharing personal data with ICE/DHS for immigration enforcement. Stated "fear of immigration enforcement should not stop people from seeking healthcare or housing." Advocates for immigrant protections and pathways to citizenship rather than mass deportation.

Cosponsored H.R. 4734 (119th Congress) to protect Social Security benefits and ensure continued effective SSA operation. Stated she will "continue to advance legislation that protects Social Security to ensure our seniors can retire with dignity." Opposed Republican proposals that would cut Social Security to fund tax breaks for billionaires.

Sits on the Judiciary Committee's subcommittee on Courts,
Intellectual Property,
Artificial Intelligence, and the Internet. As a Congressional Progressive Caucus member, aligns with stronger government oversight of technology. Limited direct AI regulation bills found, but committee position and caucus alignment indicate support for close monitoring and government approval processes.

Campaign lists "A Green New Deal and immediate climate action" as a priority, envisioning rapid renewable energy transition. Celebrated the Inflation Reduction Act as "largest-ever federal investment in tackling the existential threat of climate change." Received 100% Sierra Club scorecard (2020) and strong LCV marks. Opposed Republican efforts to roll back clean energy incentives.

Cosponsored H.R. 40 (118th Congress), the Commission to Study and Develop Reparation Proposals for African Americans. Co-authored the CROWN Act (SB 188, 2019) prohibiting hair-based racial discrimination.
As CBC Whip, champions justice and equity for Black Americans. Authored the California Abolition Act to end slavery language in the state penal code.

Lists "Innovative investment in housing" as a campaign priority. Secured $6.4M for affordable housing projects in CA-37 through FY2024 and delivered $1.6M to Butterfly's Haven for youth housing. Hosted roundtables with HUD on increasing affordable housing development and described housing as "a moral obligation."

Received an "A" rating from End Citizens United/Let America Vote Action Fund. Stated she is "committed to ending the dominance of big money in politics, safeguarding our election system, and protecting the freedom to vote." Cosponsored H.R. 4910 (Democracy Advancement and Innovation Act).

Introduced the LINE Act to protect immigrant data from ICE enforcement. Advocated for "a more humanitarian approach that gives our immigrant population the opportunity to pursue citizenship." Criticized H-1B visa restrictions as hurting the United States. Opposed family detention at the border and supported expanded legal immigration pathways.

In April 2025 Foreign Affairs subcommittee remarks, expressed concern about accurate information flows while criticizing both government censorship of federal employees and disinformation threats. Her "A" rating from End Citizens United and strong democracy-reform stance aligns with support for transparency requirements and platform accountability measures.

Cosponsored H.R. 4910 (Democracy Advancement and Innovation Act) supporting democratic process reforms. Received an "A" from End Citizens United/Let America Vote. Represents California, which already uses an independent citizens' redistricting commission. Strong voting rights advocacy and John Lewis VRA cosponsorship support independent commission model.

Cosponsored the Medicare for All Act of 2019 (H.R. 1384), a single-payer bill. Stated "Quality, affordable healthcare is a right, not a privilege." In 2025, voted NO on Trump's reconciliation bill citing cuts to Medicare and Medicaid.

Holds 100% ratings from NARAL Pro-Choice America and Planned Parenthood. Voted for the Women's Health Protection Act (H.R. 8296, 2022) and Ensuring Access to Abortion Act (H.R. 8297). Sponsored the Reproductive Data Privacy and Protection Act (H.R. 7841, March 2024) and strongly opposed the Dobbs decision.

Strong critic of Trump's sweeping tariffs. Voted to suspend normal trade relations with Russia and Belarus (H.R. 7108), showing support for targeted punitive trade measures. Generally favors multilateral trade agreements with environmental and labor standards rather than broad protectionist tariffs.

Congressional Progressive Caucus member who voted against the 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act and the 2025
One Big Beautiful Bill, characterizing it as providing tax breaks for the wealthy while cutting healthcare. Supported the Inflation Reduction Act's corporate minimum tax. Holds a 97-100% lifetime AFL-CIO score.

Voted for the Respect for Marriage Act (December 2022), calling it "landmark legislation to ensure marriage equality for all." Authored California's first-in-the-nation conversion therapy ban for minors (signed 2012). Consistently receives 100% ratings from LGBTQ advocacy organizations.

Authored California's conversion therapy ban despite religious objections. Supports the Equality Act prohibiting discrimination based on sexual orientation and gender identity without broad religious exemptions. Cosponsored H.Res.1058 (119th Congress) recognizing the duty to codify transgender rights. Consistently prioritizes civil rights over religious exemptions.

Cosponsored H.Res.1058 (119th Congress, Feb 2026), a resolution recognizing the duty to develop a Transgender Bill of Rights. Authored California's conversion therapy ban and consistently opposes anti-trans legislation. His 100% rating from LGBTQ organizations reflects support for transgender inclusion including in athletics.

Voted for all three military aid supplemental packages for Ukraine in April 2024. Voted for the Ukraine Security Assistance and Oversight Supplemental Appropriations Act (H.R. 5692) and to suspend trade relations with Russia (H.R. 7108). As a former Air Force colonel and Foreign Affairs Committee member, consistently supports continued military and economic aid.

Cosponsored the Medicare for All Act of 2019 (H.R. 1384) to expand Medicare to cover all Americans. Opposes Medicare privatization. In 2025, voted NO on Trump's reconciliation bill specifically citing it would "cut Medicare and Medicaid." Strongly criticized Republican efforts to reduce Medicaid eligibility.

Introduced the Climate Solutions Act to model national emissions reduction after California. Sponsored the Increasing Penalties for Offshore Polluters Act (H.R. 5373, Sept 2023). Opposes offshore drilling and voted against the Lower Energy Costs Act (H.R. 1, 118th Congress) which expanded fossil fuel permitting. Near-perfect LCV score.

Voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act) which includes automatic voter registration, online voter registration, and expanded early/mail voting. Voted for H.R. 4 (John R. Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act, 2021) and H.R. 5746 (Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act). Advocates ending dark money and partisan gerrymandering.

Called for ICE and CBP to "stand down" interior operations. Cosponsored the Southeast Asian Deportation Relief Act (H.R. 7608, Feb 2026) and introduced the Citizenship for Essential Workers Act. Supports DACA and pathways for long-term residents while opposing mass deportation policies.

Congressional Progressive Caucus member with 97-100% AFL-CIO score who consistently opposes cuts to Social Security. Voted against the Limit, Save, Grow Act (H.R. 2811, 2023) which included spending caps potentially affecting Social Security. Opposes privatization and supports raising taxes on higher earners to fund the program.

Congress's leading voice on AI regulation. Co-chaired the bipartisan House AI Task Force. Introduced the National AI Commission Act (2023), the Block Nuclear Launch by Autonomous AI Act (2023), the AI Fraud Deterrence Act (H.R. 10125, Nov 2024), and the Federal AI Risk Management Act. At a TEDx talk, called it "insane" not to regulate AI and stated "you always have to have a human in a loop."

Introduced the Climate Solutions Act modeled on California's climate targets. Cosponsored the Addressing Climate Financial Risk Act (H.R. 7246, Jan 2026). Calls climate change an "existential threat" and voted for the Inflation Reduction Act's clean energy investments. Authored California legislation mandating alternative-fuel fleet vehicles (AB 236). Near-perfect LCV score.

Voted for the George Floyd Justice in Policing Act. Supported California SCA 5 to allow race-conscious university admissions. Cosponsored the Sikh American Anti-Discrimination Act (H.R. 7100, Jan 2026). Consistently votes against Republican efforts to slash civil rights enforcement funding and calls for systemic reform.

Secured nearly $200 million in funding to address affordable-housing development and homelessness per Progressive Voters Guide. Actively works on homelessness in Los Angeles. Consistently votes for HUD funding and against Republican proposals to cut federal housing programs.

Voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act) which includes strict limits on corporate and dark money, public financing through small-donor matching, and mandatory disclosure. Supported the Freedom to Vote Act's campaign finance provisions. Consistently advocates limiting dark money and corporate influence in politics.

Introduced the Citizenship for Essential Workers Act and cosponsored the Southeast Asian Deportation Relief Act (H.R. 7608, Feb 2026). Supports DACA and the DREAM Act. Called for ICE to stand down interior operations. Supports comprehensive immigration reform with pathways to citizenship but does not advocate open borders.

Introduced the AI Disclosure Act requiring AI-generated content to include disclaimers. Pushed for transparency in digital political advertising through H.R. 1. Sponsored the AI Fraud Deterrence Act (H.R. 10125, Nov 2024) targeting AI-generated misinformation. Favors mandatory fact-checking and algorithm transparency while balancing First Amendment concerns.

Voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act) in both the 116th and 117th Congresses, which requires all states to establish independent citizens' redistricting commissions. Called for ending partisan gerrymandering in his support for the Freedom to Vote Act. Represents California which uses an independent citizens' commission model he supports extending nationally.

Voted against the American Rescue Plan Act (March 2021) which expanded ACA subsidies. Voted for the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA, signed July 2025) imposing Medicaid work requirements without extending enhanced ACA premium tax credits. Sponsors the Preventive Health Savings Act (H.R. 4464, 2025) focused on market-based cost savings.

Supported the overturning of Roe v. Wade, stating the Constitution does not mention abortion explicitly so states may outlaw it. SBA Pro-Life America states he "voted consistently to defend the lives of the unborn" including opposing taxpayer funding for abortion. Frames it as a states' rights issue rather than pushing a complete federal ban.

Generally votes with the Republican conference on trade matters and supported Trump's trade agenda. Voted for reconciliation packages consistent with the Trump administration's approach. His consistent party alignment and district interests suggest support for increased tariffs on unfair trade practices, particularly with China.

Endorsed by the Howard Jarvis Taxpayers Association. Voted for the Limit Save Grow Act of 2023 (H.R. 2811). Voted for the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (signed July 2025), which permanently extended 2017 TCJA individual tax rate cuts, expanded the SALT deduction cap, and added new deductions for tips, overtime, and auto loans.

Voted FOR the Respect for Marriage Act on both July 19, 2022, and December 8, 2022, one of only 39-47 House Republicans to do so. Stated: "As an ardent advocate for limited government, I do not feel that government should be empowered to dictate the terms of a marriage." Supports nationwide recognition with religious liberty protections.

While he voted for the Respect for Marriage Act with its religious liberty protections, his overall profile aligns with allowing faith-based exemptions. The final Respect for Marriage Act he supported ensured religious organizations would not lose tax-exempt status. Heritage Action lifetime score of 74% reflects general conservative alignment on religious freedom issues.

The OBBBA he supported (signed July 2025) included provisions banning Medicaid from funding gender-affirming care. Republicans in the 118th and 119th Congresses broadly supported the Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (H.R. 734), which requires sports teams to be based on biological sex. His consistent Republican conference voting on cultural issues supports this position.

Voted FOR the Ukraine Democracy Defense Lend-Lease Act (2022), the 2022
Ukraine Supplemental, and H.R. 5692 Ukraine Security Assistance (2023). Voted AGAINST amendments to cut Ukraine aid. However, voted AGAINST standalone H.R. 8035 Ukraine Security Supplemental (April 2024). Republicans For Ukraine graded him a "C." Overall pattern through 2023 supported continued aid.

Voted for the OBBBA (signed July 2025) which included Medicaid work requirements for able-bodied adults, more frequent eligibility redeterminations, and cuts estimated to reduce coverage for millions. Works with 60 Plus Association on "protecting Social Security and Medicare for seniors" but in the context of reform and sustainability rather than expansion.

Voted for H.R. 1, the Lower Energy Costs Act (2023), which expanded fossil fuel drilling and streamlined permitting. Voted for the OBBBA which rolled back IRA clean energy credits, rescinded clean energy funds, mandated at least 4 million acres of coal leasing on federal land, and created the Energy Dominance Financing Program.

Voted against certifying Arizona's and Pennsylvania's 2020 electoral votes on January 6, 2021, citing "unilateral changes to election law." Voted against H.R. 1/For the People Act which would have expanded voting access. Aligns with the Republican position favoring voter ID requirements and updated voter rolls.

His official website states "Illegal immigration threatens our national security and the safety of our communities." Opposed California's Sanctuary State law as an Assemblyman. In February 2026, toured the California City Immigration Processing Center emphasizing enforcement. Voted for OBBBA which included $175 billion in border/immigration enforcement funding.

Works with the 60 Plus Association on "protecting Social Security and Medicare for seniors." The OBBBA he supported includes a $6000 bonus deduction for seniors to effectively eliminate Social Security income tax for 88% of seniors. No evidence supports privatization; his position aligns with preserving the program through small adjustments.

Co-chaired the bipartisan House Task Force on AI with Rep. Lieu, producing a December 2024 report. Stated "regulation can't be rushed" (Fast Company, 2024) and criticized Europe's comprehensive approach. Advocates sector-specific regulation over broad frameworks. Sponsored the AI Whistleblower Protection Act (H.R. 3460) and favors federal preemption of state AI laws with light-touch oversight.

Voted for H.R. 1 Lower Energy Costs Act (2023) expanding fossil fuel production. Voted for the OBBBA which rolled back IRA clean energy tax credits, ended EV purchase credits, and rescinded clean energy program funding. While he co-sponsors some climate-related bills, his voting pattern overwhelmingly favors market forces and energy production over government-directed climate action.

Voted to denounce socialism (H.Con.Res. 9, 2023). Heritage Action lifetime score of 74% reflects conservative alignment. No specific civil rights legislation identified where he broke from the Republican conference. General stance favors maintaining existing protections without expanding federal enforcement.

As a
California Assemblyman, expressed interest in housing but emphasized fixing existing programs over creating new ones and limiting long-term funding commitments. His general philosophy that "greater regulation fundamentally threatens economic growth" and campaign emphasis on reducing regulations suggest a deregulatory approach to housing.

Voted against H.R. 1/For the People Act (2021) which would have imposed stricter campaign finance disclosure requirements and limited dark money. No specific campaign finance legislation sponsored. His small-government philosophy and Republican conference alignment suggests support for reducing restrictions on political spending.

His website states "a robust system of legal immigration is vital to our society" but emphasizes border security and enforcement. Opposed the NO BAN Act (April 2021) limiting presidential immigration restrictions. Opposed California's Sanctuary State law. Supports E-Verify and favors enforcement while maintaining legal channels for high-skilled workers.

His AI policy work emphasizes innovation over regulation. Expressed concern about AI's "potential to influence human behavior" (Washington Post, 2023) but favors industry self-regulation and sector-specific approaches over government-mandated content moderation. His limited-government philosophy and opposition to broad regulatory frameworks prioritize free speech protections.

California already uses an independent citizens' redistricting commission, and Obernolte represents a district drawn by that commission. No specific federal redistricting legislation or statements found. His moderate conservative profile and experience with California's independent commission suggest acceptance of bipartisan processes rather than pure partisan control.

Co-sponsored AB 2200 (2024), the California Guaranteed Health Care for All Act (CalCare), to create a comprehensive universal single-payer system for all California residents regardless of immigration status. Also voted for legislation expanding Medi-Cal eligibility to low-income adults regardless of immigration status.

Co-authored SB 1142 (2022) establishing state-funded abortion services including travel expenses for out-of-state patients. Voted for SCA 10 to enshrine reproductive freedom in the California constitution. Voted for SB 245 eliminating abortion cost-sharing and AB 2134 establishing the Reproductive Health Equity Fund.

Authored AB 71 (2021), the "Bring California Home Act," which would raise taxes on corporations making over $5 million in annual profits to generate $2.4 billion/year for homelessness programs, explicitly targeting Trump-era corporate tax breaks. Voted in 2019 to increase income taxes on Californians earning more than $1 million per year.

Authored AB 1163 (2023), the Gender Identity and Inclusivity Act expanding LGBTQ data collection. Voted for legislation establishing privacy rights for gender-affirming care and housing incarcerated transgender individuals by gender identity. Her 98/100 progressive Courage Score reflects full support for marriage equality with full benefits.

Consistent pattern of voting for LGBTQ anti-discrimination protections without religious exemptions, including bills protecting gender-affirming care from out-of-state legal challenges. Supported prohibiting employer discrimination for reproductive choices (AB 569) and gender-neutral retail requirements. Her 98/100 progressive Courage Score reflects prioritizing civil rights over religious exemptions.

Authored AB 1163 (Gender Identity and
Inclusivity Act, 2023). Voted for legislation allowing incarcerated transgender individuals to be housed by gender identity and supported privacy and healthcare protections for gender-affirming care. California law (AB 1266, 2013) already allows students to participate in sports matching their gender identity, which Rivas has shown no opposition to.

Co-sponsored AB 2200 (CalCare, 2024) to create universal single-payer healthcare subsuming Medi-Cal. Voted to expand Medi-Cal to low-income adults regardless of immigration status. As a congresswoman, opposed Medicaid cuts noting "In my district, over half of the constituents rely on Medicaid for healthcare - 52%."

As Chair of the
Assembly Natural Resources Committee, negotiated California's 2022 legislative climate package including SB 1137 banning new oil drilling permits within 3200 feet of homes and schools. Earned a 97% lifetime score from California Environmental Voters. Consistent pattern supports stopping new permits while not advocating an immediate total ban on existing drilling.

Voted for AB 860 (2020) authorizing vote-by-mail, AB 646 (2020) allowing individuals on parole for felony convictions to vote, and supported same-day voter registration. Also voted for SB 314 (2023) establishing an independent Citizens' Redistricting Commission in Sacramento County.

Voted to expand Medi-Cal to immigrants regardless of status and supported prohibiting CA prisons from working with ICE contractors. As a congresswoman, visited
Adelanto Detention Center, called ICE raids "indiscriminate," and introduced the INFORM Act after a constituent was transferred without family notification. Supports pathways to citizenship and opposes detaining non-criminal immigrants.

Introduced the "AI for ALL Act" in Congress focused on AI education and access. Offered an amendment to remove a Republican 10-year AI moratorium, stating "We should be learning from how states address current and new challenges from AI systems, not blocking their progress." Supported California's 2024 laws (AB 2655, AB 2839) regulating deepfakes in elections. Favors basic safety testing and state-level regulation while promoting innovation.

Chaired the Assembly Natural Resources Committee and negotiated the 2022 legislative climate package. Authored multiple climate bills including AB 2076 (Extreme Heat and Community Resilience) and AB 1757 (carbon sequestration targets). Supported AB 1279 (carbon neutrality by 2045 with 85% direct emissions reduction) and SB 1020 (90% clean energy by 2035). Earned 97% lifetime California Environmental Voters score.

Supported multiple police accountability bills prohibiting certain restraint techniques and banning tear gas on protestors. Authored AB 1163 (LGBTQ Disparities Reduction Act expansion). Member of the Congressional Progressive Caucus and Hispanic Caucus. Consistent pattern of strengthening enforcement and addressing systemic discrimination.

Made homelessness a signature issue. Authored AB 71 (Bring California Home Act, $2.4B/year for homelessness), AB 1845 (first State Office on Homelessness), AB 799 (Homelessness Accountability and
Results Act, 2023), and AB 1318 (Urban Infill Housing CEQA exemption expansion). Secured over $68.9 million for her district including housing and community services.

Supported expanding Medi-Cal to immigrants regardless of status, authorizing DREAMers in
County Central Committees, and prohibiting CA prisons from working with ICE contractors. As a congresswoman, conducted oversight visits to detention facilities and introduced the INFORM Act. Supports creating pathways to citizenship and improving the asylum process.

California passed AB 2655 (requiring platforms to remove or label deceptive AI-generated election content) and AB 2839 (prohibiting deceptive campaign content) during her Assembly tenure. As a congresswoman, fought against the Republican AI moratorium that would have blocked states from regulating AI misinformation. Approach favors mandating transparency and fact-checking for election content.

Voted for SB 314 (2023) to establish an independent Citizens' Redistricting Commission in
Sacramento County, extending the citizen-led model. California already uses an independent Citizens Redistricting Commission at the state level, and Rivas's vote to expand this model demonstrates consistent support for non-partisan redistricting through independent citizens' commissions.
